title: Information we can share with licensed immigration advisers and lawyers :: Immigration New Zealand
content:
## Information we can share with licensed immigration advisers and lawyers
Our Customer Service Centre and Privacy Team can share with licensed immigration advisers (LIAs) and lawyers.
If you are a licensed immigration adviser (LIA) or lawyer, you can ask us for information about a client, and the visas they have been given or have applied for, either by:
- calling our Customer Service Centre, or
- sending our Privacy Team a request.
Before you are given any information, you must confirm that:
- you are representing the visa applicant
- your details are listed on the Law Society website.
The New Zealand Law Society Te Kāhui Ture o Aotearoa (NZLS)

## The type of information we can share with you
We can share with you details about:
- the visa a client currently holds
- applications they have completed
- their previous visas
- the processing of any current applications
- medical examinations they have had
- police certificates they have provided
- their travel movements
- word-for-word (verbatim) copies of any file notes
- how to contact Immigration New Zealand staff who are dealing with their application.
Whether you should be calling our Customer Service Centre, or sending a request to our Privacy Team will depend on the type of enquiry you have.

### Visa currently held by the client
Call the Customer Service Centre for information about the:
- visa start and expiry dates
- current eVisa, if this is available.
Some older visas are not available from the Customer Service Centre. You can, however, send a request to the Privacy Team for confirmation of your client's visa status.

### Applications completed
Call the Customer Service Centre if you wish to:
- be sent a current eVisa, if it is available (regardless of date of decision)
- discuss the decision letter content and any relevant immigration instructions
- be advised of the timeframes and process for reconsideration requests
- be resent decision letters for previous applications.
Send a request to the Privacy Team for:
- copies of correspondence to, or from, Immigration New Zealand staff
- interview transcripts or summaries
- information about records of visa applications.

### Previous visas held, or application history
Call the Customer Service Centre to get previous application information, such as:
- ANZSCO codes used in a visa assessment.
They can also tell you if your client has ever:
- been unlawful in New Zealand
- been issued with a Deportation Liability Notice.
Send a request to the Privacy Team for:
- periods of time the client was unlawful
- detailed reasons for previous visa declines, such as copies of file notes and/or assessment templates
- previous s61 decision records.

### Application processing
Call the Customer Service Centre for:
- visa application processing updates (if the application has been allocated to an immigration officer)
- details of letters sent to the client (or you as their LIA or lawyer) and get them re-sent if available.
Send a request to the Privacy Team for:
- copies of Immigration New Zealand correspondence (such as emails from an immigration officer)
- interview transcripts
- copies of file notes.
Call the Customer Service Centre for:
- the date and type of medical
- the date of a chest X-ray
- the outcome of any medical assessment (acceptable standard of health or otherwise)
- any further medical requirements with the next application
- the eMedical reference (NZER) number
- a copy of the eMedical certificate which shows any further medical requirements requested with the next application.
Call the Customer Service Centre to give you police certificate:
Send a request to the Privacy Team for copies of police certificates.
Call the Customer Service Centre for:
- arrival and departure dates
- complete travel movements.
Complete travel movements can also be requested from the Privacy Team.

### Verbatim (word for word) copies of file notes
Send a request to the Privacy Team for verbatim copies of file notes.
The Customer Service Centre cannot provide these.

### Update contact details
Call the Customer Service Centre to update a client's:
- LIA or lawyer details.

### Immigration New Zealand (INZ) staff contact details
Mobile phone numbers for INZ staff cannot be shared.
Call the Customer Service Team to get an INZ immigration officer or immigration manager's:
The Customer Service Team cannot share Immigration New Zealand staff contact details for applications that have not yet been allocated to an immigration officer, whether they are paper-based or submitted online.
If a visa application has yet to be allocated to an immigration officer, you can request for it to be allocated urgently.
Escalation for urgent applications
Request or change your personal information
